body.notes: Participation in IETF meetings constitutes agreement to the Note Well, which permits recording, transcription, and public archival of proceedings. See also BCP 78 (RFC 5378) and BCP 79 (RFC 8179) for IPR policies.

transcript.text: okay thanks oh that seemed to work better how about now can people hear me okay now we're here great thanks jari and uh and uh thanks uh alessandro for being here uh to help us out with all that the muteco side of things um a lot of great improvements to the tool i had a chance to test drive some of them i think it's going to work really well for us but sometimes takes a little getting used to i also want to recognize and say thanks to uh barry my co-chair he's on here too i see and uh and to the rest of you there's still a number of people joining in so i'm gonna take just a couple minutes and probably start at five past the hour on the actual material we will be using the the queue to kind of control who's who's speaking you can put yourself in the queue if you have something you want to say and then i'll invite you to unmute your mic and uh if you're up for it you can send your video that can be helpful as well we don't have any other presentations planned other than the one i'll be showing which won't take um it won't take the whole time certainly won't take two hours i don't think it'll even take i'll take less than half an hour so most of the time will be for for q a um you can jump in with questions at any point though um you don't have to wait till the end for questions but we'll certainly have a lot of time for questions at the end and uh and to help people get set up and one of the things we'll do as well as we have a large number of projects more than 20 projects and a lot of the the champions for the various projects are here and then if if any of them have anything they want to say or if any of you or just want to be able to ask some quick clarifying questions about a specific project we can do that as well and i see it's it's five after and so in the interest of time whether you're getting up early for this or staying up late uh we'll get started as you can see it is being recorded and that will be available for everyone so again welcome to the hackathon as you can see it will be it's starting now and it will be running all week as has become our kind of new format when we switch to running as a virtual or online only hackathon so what you see here the decision welcome to the hackathon this was actually the last time we were physically in prague it also happened to be our largest hackathon in terms of overall participation i think we had about 400 people that eventually crammed into this room and to the room outside in some ways you'll be more comfortable at this one in the comforts of your own home uh it's going to look a little bit more like this um we'll have the gather space if you haven't tried that out yet i invite you to uh to give it a try we'll talk about a little bit more how to use it my understanding is there may be a password added to that and a kind of fun password not in prague it doesn't seem to be an effect yet but that's something that may go into effect during the course of this week or next week so if you're prompted for a password this should work when i tried last night and this morning there still wasn't a password needed but anyways i put it in there just in case um oh i also forgot to mention i have my uh my t-shirt from the first time we were in prague which was uh with the hackathon in prague that was uh six years ago at iatf 93 and that was the one where that was an important hackathon because i think that's where it was the second time second hackathon and that's where we really saw the intf as a community kind of rallying behind this idea of having a dedicated time and space to come together and and work on code and things related to code or things work on code related to ietf standards and so whether we're meeting in person or we're meeting like this uh in more of a an online space the goals are still the same right we're really coming together to advance that the pace and relevance of the the work that we're doing in the ietf so there's a lot of great work that goes on around the specifications and that's how most people know us is by the rfcs that come out but it's really great if we can actually work together on some code related to that whether it be testing out new ideas for protocols that are still in development or writing an implementation of an existing rfc and getting interoperability to work better or just making it easier for people to make use of all the great uh protocols that that we have to find so that's all great stuff and really helps the work of the ietf not only complete sooner but with higher quality and to you know be put into practice more quickly and more effectively we also really look to attract i'd say a whole different set of people who might typically be attracted to collaborating on uh standards the standard when you think of standards people you typically think of documents and in the ietf we uh you know whether it be github or email where we're focusing on the text that's in those documents and the ideas but as we all know it's very important if we want the standards to be developed and used that we interact with developers and so we're really trying to reach out more to that developer set and to just bring new people who uh maybe have great development skills and are very passionate about the internet and using it making it work better or security or whatever their their passion is to get them interested in the ietf and to realize hey there's um interesting work going on and and ways for them to contribute uh that maybe are a little bit um more to their liking and so hopefully we do that as well this is also a great way to match up new people to the ietf with people who have been working on ietf standards for a number of years and that's one of the big pluses i would say and goals of the hackathon so in addition to working on your code be sure to interact with people whether that be in the gather space or other things that get set up for your team to work together as with everything in the ietf we operate under the notewell now usually uh in the ietf a lot of the work is around the drafts and and the presentations at an ietf meeting um here it's mostly around the code but we still have some presentations and so for example um when you do your presentation at the end some we'll talk about how you can share your results and what you did that all falls under the note well so if you're not familiar with the notewell have a look at it it'll help you with the hackathon and know what you're agreeing to it'll also help you next week when hopefully you stick around for the ietf meeting for the code itself as you might expect really what governs the code you're working on is the license associated with it in this example i'm showing it it's the apache 2.0 license so the code that you're working on is not unless it's explicitly states that it is it's not an ietf contribution it's not ietf code the rules governing it are those of the project you're working on and the license related to that code okay now to the more fun and exciting stuff the projects so all the projects are hopefully up on the wiki now i see a few got added over the last few days so if you took a look early last week um you may want to take another quick look because a number of new projects are there there's more than 20 there and um so i if you haven't already figured it out that's a good first place to go take a quick look at what's there see what's of interest to you you can see all the champions have identified themselves and a way to contact them so even if they're not on this call right now you can reach out to them via whatever mechanism they provided on the wiki and if you have a hard time connecting with anyone you can always reach out to me i'll try to help but also we have this page this wiki page lost and found and uh when i made this slide at least no one had entered anything there yet but the idea is throughout the course of this week if um you know you have some things you want to work on but they don't seem to line up really well with any of the projects you can add yourself to the skills to offer and similarly if you're championing a project or working on a project and you guys really need someone with some specific you know skill say to code something up or run right the front end for a web app or the back end or whatever you can put that here so that's just a good way to help people connect okay so uh you're all here for the kickoff which you can see we do have the two hours and as i mentioned i don't expect us to need all of that um as soon as this is over though all the teams are uh free to go and work on their projects on their own timescape schedule and the reason we've done this is because of time zones and uh you know just the fact that it's hard to get people to meet when you're all over the world at different places different things going on we figure okay we'll have the whole week and so teams are free to meet you know for blocks of time every single day or just on certain days but the best way to see when a team is meeting and for a team to advertise when they're meeting is to put on the team schedule and i'm showing that wiki page here um the team champions i highly recommend if you haven't yet uh to go and add some information there i i put some for the chairs i see the quick team um put how to get in contact with them and then you can take it from there right as to how the team's going to work and you can update this during the course of the week to add new new uh new times and new ways to meet then on friday at the end of this week at basically the same time as the kickoff that's when we'll be having the closing now for this one i i expect it will take close to the full two hours of time but that really depends on how many teams present their results and we'll talk later on about how the results presentations work but really the whole idea of the closing is to come together now the week's done and for people to show hey this is what we got this is what we accomplished and and so every team has the opportunity to present you don't have to present but it's certainly uh very helpful and i'll provide some more information on that in later slides so at the beginning i gave you kind of a snapshot of the what the entire gather space looked like um and that's what you're seeing here when you first come into the space it actually uh it gives you a handy little tutorial about how to use gather and interact in the space in general once you're inside of it over on the right hand side and the lower lower right corner there that's where the hackathon space actually is and so you can go on over to there you can see my tiny little character here about to enter and as you enter the hackathon space it you actually enter into a dedicated space for the hackathon where it's a little bit easier to interact with just the people in the hackathon space if that's what you want to do an example of that there's this podium up here where if we were meeting in person i'd probably be standing there delivering from this presentation from there but if i was to go to that podium i would actually have the ability to um communicate with all of you in like a conference setting i may try that out at some point just to see how well it works if there's a lot of us there it'll be interesting to see how well it works but um anyways that's one example now you notice there are many different tables here and some are labeled to be used for one of the ietf areas and some just have letters during the course of this week really the main focus of this entire space is to serve the hackathon so you know you're welcome to use one of the letter tables but you can also use one of the other tables that that's for one of the areas that's perfectly fine as well you can see there's these sort of more informal uh areas when you enter into one of these basically anyone who enters into one of these tables and takes a seat or anyone who goes into one of these lounge little uh couch areas will be able to be in sort of a video conference with the other people who are also there so it just helps people to um collaborate in a virtual environment and so i you know recommend that you test that out one of the really cool things is you'll notice on most of the tables if not all of them they have either one of these notepads which is um you can go up to it and interact with it by pressing x and then you it pops up a cody md like shared document that everyone who's at that table or you know anyone who comes up and interacts with it can can use so that's a handy way for your team to like share notes and those types of things other tables have a whiteboard and then it's more of an interactive whiteboard where you can draw things out or do whatever you want to do so both mechanisms are there you may want to choose your table depending on which one you think you'd be more likely to use those came in real handy at the last hackathon and i'm sure they will at this one as well other than that there are uh there's a complete guide that's been put out for all ietf uh 110 participants and there is a little section there on the on gather i believe it's going to be built out in a bit more detail but really i think you'll find the um just a little tutorial at the beginning get you up to speed pretty quickly it's not that complex a tool and other than that just go and experiment a little bit i think you'll find it pretty intuitive and easy to use and hopefully a great way to collaborate uh during the course of this week and next week for the ietf meeting okay one one bit of bad news for those of you who uh didn't know this one of the cool things we've been able to do in the past is provide a way to connect in to the ietf network remotely and that helped if you wanted to work on certain technologies that really required that sort of being all connected into the same layer 2 switch or or you need some dedicated network requirements and that's always been provided at sort of a as part of an experimental best effort type of thing we ran into some snags with that this year or for this hackathon unfortunately there's a few of you who got your request in early and were actually able to um to meet your requests but we had to um uh we're not accepting any new requests right now just due to a number of circumstances including helping out with with covid and the distribution of the vaccine uh being kind of a priority where some of the folks who are going to volunteer time don't have time to focus on this so um anyways it's it's a great thing we're not giving up on it we plan to offer this capability not only for um online hackathons like this one but even for in-person hackathons to help um with administering the network and allowing remote participants to work more effectively with their teams so hopefully this won't be a big problem for those of you who um if you were planning on using it hopefully you got your request in early and we were able to get you set up otherwise if you haven't been set up yet you know my apologies okay project presentation so i mentioned this we uh we do these at the end of the hackathon during that closing session which is on friday at the same time as this this kicked off and the idea there is to recap here's what our team was trying to solve and here's what we achieved and here are the things we learned along the way um these are very short presentations and so the goal is not to dive into the the full extent the full you know deep dive into it it's you know think about your audience that it's a technical audience but maybe not an audience that is familiar with your exact project or the working group drafts that you're you know related to what you're doing so they have a good grasp of ietf technology overall but maybe not your specific area we really want to know like like what did you learn what did you achieve uh what are you going to feed back into your working group like did you find out that the working group giraffes are right on target or did you find some problems or something's missing or some things that you think should be changed uh you know those types of things are handy to know we're also very interested in as i mentioned at the beginning kind of extending the branching out the ietf community and interacting with with new people with new communities so if you're working on an open source project that's maybe housed outside of the ietf or you're working on something related to another sdo perhaps some yang models that while the itf to find the yang models you're working uh sorry to find that the yang language and netconf and rescoff perhaps the yang models themselves are being defined in another sdo and you're working on that these are just all examples that i think are interesting for us to know so we can see how our community that you know is expanded beyond just what we would typically think of at an ivf meeting based on the number of people who want to present um their projects um we'll decide how long they can be but my guess is as a good rule of thumb and this is what happened to the last two ietfs we were able to allow five-minute presentations and we will be pretty strict in fact there's some new tools and meet echo to help us to be strict where we can set a timer and we'll make sure that we keep to time so that everyone gets a chance to present and you're basically presenting within me techo much like i am now sharing your presentation the way we facilitate that is using the ietf hackathon github organization if you haven't seen this already it's a place where well for one it's where we keep all of our presentations related to the hackathon so you'll see a project for iatf109 there's one for ietf110 that's where this kickoff presentation exists so if you want to download it you can it's also where a lot of the hackathon projects exist you can see this one here exists there so you don't have to put your projects here but it is kind of a handy way if you don't already have a home somewhere else um we're very happy to create a repo here for you to house the code for your project so if you want that let me know reach out to me and i will create a new repo for you but for everyone who plans to present in the at the end of the hackathon or even if you don't want to present but you just want to capture your results for everyone else to see which i highly recommend that you capture the results regardless of whether anyone's available to present on friday the idea is that you would upload those presentations into the ietf 110 project presentations so if you go into that uh that repo uh and you go to the readme that's what i'm showing here there's some instructions about that the important thing that i want to point out right now is in order to in order to make this just work a little bit easier what i'd like to do is give everyone the ability to you know to commit directly into this repo and in order for that to happen you basically need to be a member of the ietf hackathon github org so if you've been added for another itf hackathon you're fine you don't need to do it again but if you're not a member you need to get added and the way to do that is to send an email to hackathonchairs.itf.org but make sure on github that your github account has your name because that just helps us identify who you are because sometimes it's really tough with um user ids which can be a bit cryptic and also please enable two-factor authentication it's probably a good practice regardless but you know it just helps us keep people from hacking into the the itf github so would appreciate that and actually require that before we will add you in and the other thing is if you're planning on presenting which i hope you do uh the sooner you do this like if you do it today that's great get yourself added if you're not there already uh don't wait till friday or even thursday night because with time zones and other things you know things get busy i may miss something we may not get you added so so don't wait till last minute please you can upload your presentation pretty late but get yourself all set up and make sure that you're able to upload it early on and then like you know like what you can always do with github you can upload an initial version of your presentation and then upload another one there's no problem with that we have a couple templates that you are welcome to use you don't have to use these we just make them available and a lot of people do find it useful for for your project presentations there's one available in powerpoint format which is the same very similar to what we've made available in the past thanks to benson we also have one in uh html format which is much lighter weight um kind of more code like i would say and certainly takes up a lot less space in general when you when you upload them um and so you're welcome to use that template um as you can see where we you know we're happy to add more templates we're happy to have them be improved over over time both of them are fairly simple both of them can probably be better if you have some suggestions you can always send us a pull request to make those better too but anyways they're there just to help you get started and hopefully uh put your your project presentation together with like minimal effort on the how to do it and you can just focus on the important things you gathered or that you learned which is really the important thing right so hopefully you find those useful um then before i open it up to questions um i want to just say a huge thanks to i can i can is a sponsor for the the hackathon our cost structure is different when we're not meeting in person uh but you know there are still costs associated with this and all the online tools we use and just very happy that um icann um has been a sponsor in the past with a hackathon and sponsored us again for the ietf 110 hackathon and um if you or your company or someone you know might be interested in being a sponsor for the hackathon going forward we'd love to get in touch you can just send something to the hackathon chairs email address and we'll put you in touch with the right people and so with that i'm going to open it up for questions happy to to get any of those and after an initial set of questions then uh the last thing we'll do is kind of open it up for any project champions that just want to share a bit more information about what they're doing but i'd like to get any questions first so if you do have a question um maybe make your way to the uh the mic to the queue enter in there and i'll allow you to uh i'll give you the four i'm not seeing any questions yet maybe maybe that was really clear maybe a lot of you have uh uh attended the hackathon before um what i'll do then is um now if there are any um any champions of projects who want to share something about their project um feel free to jump in the queue for that if they're someone who's just you know has a question about a specific project that's fine as well we'll open it up for any other questions people may have there is one thing i think i didn't mention and that was the schedule i want to show people that so while we're waiting to see if there's any questions that's one thing i will show briefly let me go back to okay it looks like we do have a question okay okay i'll go ahead see your video do we have audio for you you should be able to enable your audio yeah i don't hear your audio yet i'll stop sharing the screen okay you should be able to share your audio right now if i understand correctly and alessandra if i'm doing something wrong and and didn't uh put paul in the allow him to speak you can you can jump in at least i still don't hear you okay while we're getting that sorted out let me try this i'm going to share the um i'm going to share my screen hopefully okay so this is the hopefully you're seeing this wow there we go okay so this is the results presentation page within the wiki and uh you can see we have one project already listed here um and i would encourage you with this as well you don't have to have your presentation ready you just this is just the way to say that yes you do want to present and you can find it if you're on the hackathon wiki and you go down into the meeting materials here there is a link for results presentation schedule if you click on that it'll take you to this page and anyone can add themselves to this anyone can add anything to the to the wiki you will need an account and there are some instructions there on how to set that up but those are freely available and tied to your data tracker account so if you are on this presentation now you should be set up for doing that as well yeah paul sorry we weren't able to get your audio working but yeah perhaps if you hadn't granted access okay we'll try again now looks like you should be able to send audio and video we can see your video no still still not hearing you though seems to be some problem with your mic and alessandra is that or lorenzo asked in the chat when you see the mic lines moving under your name when you talk i can see those for example online whenever i speak there's a little blue lines right right underneath my uh my profile picture and they kind of go up and down as my voice does yeah if anyone else has any questions um the muteco team is working with paul to get get that straightened out any questions any project pitches this is your big chance to recruit people much harder to do i think in a virtual environment at least it's a little bit more challenging than when you're right there in person and someone can just walk up but uh you know we do have the whole week and that's that's one good thing and like i said if uh look at the wiki if if um if you're trying to figure out a project to join there's the lost and found then you can always reach out to me you can also send something to the hackathon list but that's perfectly fine and then the other thing is go and gather and see if you find the people there once we drop off of this kickoff session i'll probably go and play around with the gather space a little bit it's uh if you haven't really uh if you couldn't tell from the slides or if you haven't been in it yet it's all redesigned from iatf 109. there are some things that are similar but it certainly has a lot of new capabilities to it much like me techo just improving uh dramatically with lots of new functionality for each ietf meeting which is great to see and i'm sure it's going to be really beneficial to us even when we do start meeting in person again which i hope happens pretty soon okay uh niels hi yeah how are you exciting um we will be working with people from uh uh from new york from new york state university with people from uc berkeley university of amsterdam and also with people from the university of glasgow on the big bang project on quantitative emailing list analysis in the big bang project we already have a pretty nice code base working we can ingress a lot of email we also got working with with the data tracker and we're doing a lot of analysis on uh discussion uh about standards and their adoption so if people are interested feel free to join especially people uh working on python graphic people working on visualization we're also trying to make the tool more accessible to get more people because we're getting a lot of people asking us how the tool works and perhaps branching out a bit into knowledge graphs so if people are interested in that feel free to join we will be here all three way all three days in a wide range of time zones so get your coffee brewing and we are here for you great well thanks um is there um i don't know if you added anything into the the team schedule yet but in terms of exactly how people should reach out to you whether that just be with an email or if you're going to have a i don't know a webex session or something or if you're using gather that'd be good to know i'll add our zoom room fantastic okay anyone else if we don't get any more questions or uh project uh kind of announcements um we'll let people go i'm sure most of you just want to get back to uh to working on your code or other other things you need to prepare okay uh and niels you're still in the queue but i think you're you're welcome to speak again if you like okay all right well i don't see anyone in the queue i think we pretty much covered everything so so thanks to all of you for joining uh we'll wrap up at at this point and um be available throughout the week on uh you can contact me directly and gather um on barry as well at the hackathon chairs uh list if you want to just talk to us that way you know or via the hackathon at itf.org list if you want to reach out to everyone um and then uh you know so they'll probably be some updates during the course of the week we'll share those via the the hackathon list other than that let me go back up and kind of wrap up with just a quick look at our agenda again just to make sure everyone knows the timing of things but pretty pretty simple um working on at your own pace and filling out things in the team schedule i highly recommend that and then on friday at this same time is when we'll come together using meet techo again to do the presentations and if you would like to do a presentation whether you're going to present it live or not uh please upload it to the uh to the github org that was mentioned here but if you do want to present live which i highly encourage someone from the team it doesn't need to be the project champion make sure that you get your project and i see we have one more on there already in this list and then we will just present in order that people are on the list if you have a specific reason why you need to present out of order let me know we can we can rearrange things um or you know whatever so we can be kind of flexible but just so that people know what to expect we'll kind of plan on going in order you can plan for five minutes per project if we get more presentations than we can handle we'll probably reduce that down to say four minutes but but i think we should be good okay well thank you everyone i really appreciate you joining and i hope you have a great hackathon and really a great two weeks at ietf 110. so take care and talk to you again soon
